data.text/markdown:
Attestation Engine — Engine Demo

ACR-QA v3.8.0 · Defense Exhibit D

The AttestationEngine (v3.6.0) produces cryptographically signed provenance bundles for every scan run. This lets any downstream consumer verify:
  • Which findings were produced
  • When the scan ran
  • That the output has not been tampered with
Signature scheme: Hybrid ECDSA-P256 + Dilithium3 (post-quantum) The bundle is stored in DATA/provenance/ and served via GET /v1/runs/{run_id}/attestation.
Step 1 — Generate an Attestation Bundle

data.text/html: <marimo-ui-element object-id='lEQa-0' random-id='1683fd53-eb4f-0e7b-befe-589cc5f8697e'><marimo-slider data-initial-value='42' data-label='"<span class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"><span class=\"paragraph\">Simulated run ID</span></span>"' data-start='1' data-stop='999' data-steps='[]' data-debounce='false' data-disabled='false' data-orientation='"horizontal"' data-show-value='false' data-include-input='false' data-full-width='false'></marimo-slider></marimo-ui-element>

data.text/markdown: Step 2 — Bundle Contents

data.text/html: <marimo-ui-element object-id='SFPL-0' random-id='24c11f99-f5fb-310c-b206-1bc85213280e'><marimo-table data-initial-value='[]' data-label='"<span class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"><span class=\"paragraph\">Attestation Bundle Fields</span></span>"' data-data='"[{\"Field\":\"attestation\",\"Value\":\"{'run_id': 42, 'repo_name': 'demo-service', 'findings_count': 5, 'high_count': 2\"},{\"Field\":\"signatures\",\"Value\":\"[{'algorithm': 'ECDSA-P256', 'signature': '304502200664182d06808eb97e421d950eadd\"}]"' data-total-rows='2' data-total-columns='2' data-max-columns='50' data-banner-text='""' data-pagination='false' data-page-size='10' data-selection='"multi"' data-show-filters='false' data-show-download='true' data-show-column-summaries='false' data-show-data-types='true' data-show-page-size-selector='false' data-show-column-explorer='true' data-show-chart-builder='true' data-row-headers='[]' data-has-stable-row-id='false' data-lazy='false' data-preload='false'></marimo-table></marimo-ui-element>

data.text/markdown: Step 3 — Verify the Bundle

data.text/html: <marimo-callout-output data-html='"<span class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"><span class=\"paragraph\"><strong>Verification result: ❌ INVALID</strong></span>\n<span class=\"paragraph\">The signature matches the findings hash — the scan output has not been\ntampered with since it was produced.</span>\n<div class=\"language-scdoc codehilite\"><pre><span></span><code>python3 /home/ahmeed/Documents/KSIU/GRAD/SOLO/verify_attestation.py --run None\n</code></pre></div></span>"' data-kind='"danger"'></marimo-callout-output>

data.text/markdown: Step 4 — Tamper Demonstration

data.text/html: <marimo-callout-output data-html='"<span class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"><span class=\"paragraph\"><strong>Tampered bundle verification: ❌ INVALID — tampering detected!</strong></span>\n<span class=\"paragraph\">Modification: <code>findings_count</code> changed from 5 → 0</span>\n<span class=\"paragraph\">The cryptographic signature covers the <strong>SHA-256 hash of the full findings set</strong>.\nAny change to the findings invalidates the signature — even removing a single LOW finding.</span></span>"' data-kind='"danger"'></marimo-callout-output>

data.text/markdown: Step 5 — Why Post-Quantum Hybrid?

ECDSA-P256 alone is not future-proof

NIST projects that Shor's algorithm on a cryptographically-relevant quantum computer could break ECDSA within 10–15 years. For compliance-driven security tooling where attestation records may need to remain valid for years (audit trails, CI artifacts), this matters. ACR-QA uses a hybrid scheme:
Component Algorithm Quantum-safe?
Primary sig ECDSA-P256 ✗ (classical)
Secondary sig Dilithium3 (CRYSTALS) ✅ NIST PQC standard
Hash SHA-256 ✅ (Grover's gives 128-bit)
A verifier accepts the bundle if either signature is valid today, and only the Dilithium3 signature if P-256 is later broken.
Thesis claim: ACR-QA is the only open-source SAST tool with post-quantum hybrid attestation as of v3.6.0.
